deep_dive.title: CoMind: Understanding Collaborative Human Activity from Multiple Minds and Views
deep_dive.abstract: Human-human collaboration is a fundamental aspect of everyday life, essential to success in a wide range of goal-directed activities from household tasks to professional teamwork. While much research has focused on modeling coordination and task execution, the cognitive processes that support such collaboration, particularly Theory of Mind (the ability to infer the mental states of others), remain difficult to study in natural settings. To address this gap, we introduce a novel egocentric and exocentric video dataset capturing real-world collaboration in cooking scenarios. The dataset integrates multi-perspective video, high-quality audio, gaze tracking, and 3D scene and object scans, with annotations for shared attention to objects, social cues and interactions between agents, as well as agent-object interactions. We establish benchmarks for Joint Attention Estimation, Socially Conditioned Object Interaction Anticipation, and Collaborative Handover Prediction, enabling research on multimodal perception, proactive assistance, and collaborative planning. By providing temporally aligned, richly annotated multimodal data, CoMind facilitates the development and evaluation of AI systems capable of modeling complex social interactions and reasoning about human behaviors in collaborative environments. Our dataset and benchmarks are made available at https://comind.ethz.ch/.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multimodal dataset with gaze tracking for collaborative intent understanding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 包含眼动追踪的多模态协作意图理解数据集
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 包含眼动追踪的多模态协作意图理解数据集
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 包含眼动追踪的多模态协作意图理解数据集
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CoMind, a large egocentric and exocentric video dataset with gaze tracking, audio, and 3D scans for studying Theory of Mind and shared attention in collaborative cooking.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 人类协作中的意图理解需要推断他人心理状态，但目前缺乏自然情境数据集。本文提出CoMind数据集，包含第一/第三人称视频、高质量音频、眼动追踪和3D场景扫描，标注了共享注意和社交线索。该数据集直接支持基于多模态信号（特别是眼动）的意图预测研究，为实时意图识别提供基准。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 人类协作中的意图理解需要推断他人心理状态，但目前缺乏自然情境数据集。本文提出CoMind数据集，包含第一/第三人称视频、高质量音频、眼动追踪和3D场景扫描，标注了共享注意和社交线索。该数据集直接支持基于多模态信号（特别是眼动）的意图预测研究，为实时意图识别提供基准。

deep_dive.title: Head, Gaze, or Finger? Comparing Object Selection Techniques in Augmented Reality for People with Low Vision
deep_dive.abstract: Augmented reality (AR) can enhance visual perception for people with low vision (PLV) by overlaying multimodal information. Selection-based augmentation further allows users to flexibly choose and augment relevant information while reducing distraction and visual clutter. However, little is known about the ability and preferences of PLV in performing object selection techniques in AR, considering their potential visual and gaze control challenges. To understand what selection techniques are suitable for PLV to support selection-based AR augmentations, we conducted a mixed-methods study with 20 PLV and 18 sighted controls who performed target selection tasks using three input techniques -- head, gaze, and finger pointing with dwell-based confirmation -- in two real-world scenarios (sitting vs. on the go). We found that for PLV, gaze-based selection enabled the fastest initial pointing when sitting and comparable overall selection time to head-based selection in both scenarios; however, due to reduced gaze stability, head-based selection remained the most stable and the least mentally demanding. Uniquely, participants with central vision loss preferred finger-based selection, reporting a greater sense of control. Our results provide empirical insights into accessible AR interaction techniques and selection-based vision enhancements for PLV.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: compares gaze as input modality for object selection in AR
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 比较注视作为增强现实中物体选择的输入方式
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 比较注视作为增强现实中物体选择的输入方式
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 比较注视作为增强现实中物体选择的输入方式
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Mixed-methods study comparing head, gaze, and finger selection techniques for low vision users in AR, revealing gaze as a viable input.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过混合方法研究，对比了头、注视和手指三种输入技术在低视力人群增强现实物体选择中的表现。研究涉及20名低视力参与者和18名正常视力对照，评估了不同技术的可用性和偏好。结果表明注视输入在特定条件下具有可行性，为包容性AR交互设计提供了实证基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文通过混合方法研究，对比了头、注视和手指三种输入技术在低视力人群增强现实物体选择中的表现。研究涉及20名低视力参与者和18名正常视力对照，评估了不同技术的可用性和偏好。结果表明注视输入在特定条件下具有可行性，为包容性AR交互设计提供了实证基础。

deep_dive.title: Spontaneous eye blinks as temporal markers of internal attention
deep_dive.abstract: Eye blinks are among the largest physiological artefacts in electroencephalography and are typically removed from neural recordings. Yet their timing may carry information about cognition. Here, we asked whether the temporal distribution of spontaneous blinks across trials provides a time-resolved behavioural signature of internal attentional focusing in working memory. In Experiment 1, blink-locked EEG analyses showed that blink timing was aligned with neural activity reflecting attentional focusing on a relevant internal representation. In Experiment 2, participants remembered the same visual information across conditions, but the relevant item was revealed either early, by a cue before report, or later, at report. Blink-frequency profiles shifted accordingly, increasing after the cue when selection was possible early and after the probe when selection was delayed. Post-cue blinks in the early-selection condition were also associated with better memory performance. Thus, more generally, spontaneous blinks provide an unobtrusive chronometric signal for tracking latent cognitive processing.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: uses spontaneous blink timing to infer internal attentional focus
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用自发性眨眼时间推断内部注意焦点
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用自发性眨眼时间推断内部注意焦点
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用自发性眨眼时间推断内部注意焦点
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Demonstrates that spontaneous blink frequency profiles align with neural activity reflecting internal attention, providing a non-invasive behavioral marker for cognitive states.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过分析自发性眨眼的时序分布，发现眨眼频率与内部注意聚焦的神经活动同步。实验表明，眨眼可以作为工作记忆中注意聚焦的时序行为标记。这一发现将原本被视为噪声的眨眼转化为认知状态的高时间分辨率指标。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文通过分析自发性眨眼的时序分布，发现眨眼频率与内部注意聚焦的神经活动同步。实验表明，眨眼可以作为工作记忆中注意聚焦的时序行为标记。这一发现将原本被视为噪声的眨眼转化为认知状态的高时间分辨率指标。

quick_skim.title: Blinks are strategically coupled with head movements in unconstrained natural gaze behavior
quick_skim.abstract: Blinks are a ubiquitous yet largely unnoticed aspect of human vision, despite causing frequent interruptions of visual input that can amount to up to 10% of waking time. By leveraging a large dataset of unconstrained gaze behavior during two natural tasks, we found a novel behavioral strategy to limit the impact of blinks: blinks were strategically coupled with head movements, which minimizes information loss due to unreliable visual input during head movements. Specifically, blink probability increased with higher head velocities and showed strong temporal modulation relative to head movement onset: Blink probability was reduced before head movement initiation and then peaked during the head movement. The strength of this coupling was tailored to the individual needs of participants, with participants with higher baseline blink showing a stronger synchronization. This indicates that blinks are a part of an individually coordinated strategy when orchestrating eye and head movements during unconstrained natural behavior.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: studies natural gaze behavior and blink-head coupling, informs gaze-based interaction design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 研究自然注视行为中眨眼与头部运动的耦合，为基于注视的交互设计提供启示
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 研究自然注视行为中眨眼与头部运动的耦合，为基于注视的交互设计提供启示
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 研究自然注视行为中眨眼与头部运动的耦合，为基于注视的交互设计提供启示
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper reveals a novel behavioral strategy: blinks are strategically coupled with head movements to minimize information loss during natural gaze, providing insights for robust gaze-based HCI.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文通过分析自然任务中的大量注视数据，发现眨眼与头部运动存在策略性耦合：眨眼概率在头部运动前降低、运动期间升高，从而减少视觉信息丢失。这一发现揭示了自然注视行为中的补偿机制，为设计更鲁棒的基于注视的人机交互系统提供了行为学基础，特别是如何预测和利用眨眼模式来提升交互自然性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文通过分析自然任务中的大量注视数据，发现眨眼与头部运动存在策略性耦合：眨眼概率在头部运动前降低、运动期间升高，从而减少视觉信息丢失。这一发现揭示了自然注视行为中的补偿机制，为设计更鲁棒的基于注视的人机交互系统提供了行为学基础，特别是如何预测和利用眨眼模式来提升交互自然性。
